content.full_text:
r
L

ROBERT E. WEISS
CPIS A. KLINGERMAN
EDWARD A. THEDER
ALAN O. SEIHS
ELIA V. PIROZZI

OFFICES
ROBERT E. WEISS INCORPORATED
920 VILLAGE OAKS DRIVE
POST OFFICE BOX 3259
COVINA, CALIFORNIA 91722
(818) 967-4302
FAX (818) 967-9216

BANKRUPTCY DEPARTMENT
DIRECT DIAL
(818) 915-8907

March 6, 1991

California Fair Political
Practices Commission
Post Office Box 307
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Gentlemen:

I am the Treasurer of various controlled committees, the controlling officeholder
being Peter F. Schabarum, Supervisor of Los Angeles County, who is about to leave
office. We have a substantial amount of money in the various committees and it is
our intention to transfer the funds to a foundation which is in the process of being
formed and which we believe will be exempt pursuant to the appropriate IRS
Sections.

I recognize that the funds that are being transferred to the foundation may not be
used for the personal benefit of Supervisor Schabarum, his immediate family, or his
Treasurer. However, I would like your direction concerning the following.

1.       If portions of the funds are used for solicitation of other funds (that is,
portions of the controlled funds that will be transferred will be used for printing,
postage and office personnel to solicit additional funds), may the additional funds
that are obtained by virtue of this solicitation that was paid for by the controlled
funds, be used to reimburse Mr. Schabarum for his actual expenses, that is, travel,
etc.? and

2.       May such newly generated funds be used to pay a salary commensurate with
his future activities having to do with the foundation, its solicitations and efforts?

I would appreciate your early response.

Very truly yours,

Robert E. Weiss,

RE:W:bh/6

embedding.qa_text: The letter is from Robert E. Weiss, the Treasurer of various controlled committees, to the California Fair Political Practices Commission. The letter discusses the intention to transfer funds to a foundation that will be exempt pursuant to appropriate IRS Sections. The Treasurer seeks direction on two specific questions: 1. Whether portions of the funds used for solicitation of other funds (such as printing, postage, and office personnel) can be used to reimburse Mr. Schabarum for his actual expenses, such as travel. 2. Whether newly generated funds can be used to pay a salary commensurate with Mr. Schabarum's future activities related to the foundation, its solicitations, and efforts. The Treasurer expresses appreciation for an early response.
